channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: What a Monopoly importer learned when it tried to make things in the U.S.A.
item.description: After getting hit with tariffs for the imported board games he sells, Jonathan Silva decided to see if he could produce a version of his Monopoly game in the United States. This is what he learned.
item.content:encoded:
After getting hit with tariffs for the imported board games he sells, Jonathan Silva decided to see if he could produce a version of his Monopoly game in the United States. This is what he learned.

(Image credit: WS Game Company)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Justice Department subpoenas New York Times reporters over Air Force One reporting
item.description: The Times says federal agents turned up on the doorsteps of several of its journalists to force grand jury testimony next week over their coverage of the Air Force One plane gifted to Trump by Qatar.
item.content:encoded:
The Times says federal agents turned up on the doorsteps of several of its journalists to force grand jury testimony next week over their coverage of the Air Force One plane gifted to Trump by Qatar.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Largest housing affordability bill in decades becomes law without Trump's signature
item.description: President Trump refused to sign the bill without Congress first passing his sweeping voter ID bill.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump refused to sign the bill without Congress first passing his sweeping voter ID bill.

(Image credit: Francisco Seco)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Microsoft is doing a hard reset of its Xbox gaming division
item.description: This week, Microsoft announced they were laying off over 3,000 staff at Xbox. What will the impact be on the company? And what do these layoffs say about the gaming industry right now?
item.content:encoded: This week, Microsoft announced they were laying off over 3,000 staff at Xbox. What will the impact be on the company? And what do these layoffs say about the gaming industry right now?
item.dc:creator: Monica Nickelsburg

item.title: Gas prices are swinging. How to budget for volatility
item.description: Gas prices have fluctuated since the U.S. and Israel launched a war on Iran, which disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz and left consumers unsure of what they'll pay at the pump.
item.content:encoded:
Gas prices have fluctuated since the U.S. and Israel launched a war on Iran, which disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz and left consumers unsure of what they'll pay at the pump.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: EPA proposes weakening heavy-duty truck pollution rules
item.description: The Trump EPA calls Biden-era rules for cutting pollution from heavy trucks "unworkable." The proposed changes have been celebrated by trucking groups and denounced by environmental groups.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump EPA calls Biden-era rules for cutting pollution from heavy trucks "unworkable." The proposed changes have been celebrated by trucking groups and denounced by environmental groups.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: New aviation mechanics graduate with jobs in hand, thanks to a labor shortage
item.description: Aviation is literally soaring in the U.S., with record passenger numbers. But with a generation of mechanics set to leave the workforce, the industry needs new graduates to fill the gap.
item.content:encoded:
Aviation is literally soaring in the U.S., with record passenger numbers. But with a generation of mechanics set to leave the workforce, the industry needs new graduates to fill the gap.

(Image credit: Angus Mordant)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: Should you sign your kids up for Trump Accounts? Four things to consider
item.description: Families can still benefit even if their children aren't eligible for the free $1,000 contribution from the federal government.
item.content:encoded:
Families can still benefit even if their children aren't eligible for the free $1,000 contribution from the federal government.

(Image credit: TIMOTHY A. CLARY)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Tensions with Iran add fresh uncertainty to an already shaky global economy
item.description: Crude oil prices jumped and stock prices fell after President Trump declared an end to the ceasefire with Iran, adding fresh uncertainty to an already shaky outlook for the global economy.
item.content:encoded:
Crude oil prices jumped and stock prices fell after President Trump declared an end to the ceasefire with Iran, adding fresh uncertainty to an already shaky outlook for the global economy.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: This factory was severely short on workers. Then it offered flexible work
item.description: A GE Appliances plant in rural northwest Georgia was short hundreds of workers amid COVID-19. A flexible work option where some workers can sign up for shifts through an app has eased the pain.
item.content:encoded:
A GE Appliances plant in rural northwest Georgia was short hundreds of workers amid COVID-19. A flexible work option where some workers can sign up for shifts through an app has eased the pain.

(Image credit: Julie Holder for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu
